feeds.title: 阮一峰的网络日志
feeds.tldr: This weekly digest examines how AI's compute-driven costs may create economic inequality, contrasting with a new metric showing model capabilities doubling every seven months. It also covers recent tech industry developments, including GitHub Copilot's ad insertion controversy, and provides a curated list of open-source developer tools and AI programming resources.
highlights: AI model pricing scales with compute rather than production volume, potentially creating tiered access between wealthy and average users.
A proposed evaluation metric indicates AI capabilities are doubling every seven months based on human task completion times.
GitHub temporarily paused Copilot after it automatically inserted promotional ads into thousands of pull requests, alongside curated open-source developer tools and AI tutorials.

items.title: 科技爱好者周刊（第 391 期）：AI 的贫富分化

feeds.title: LINUX DO - 使用人工智能标记的话题
feeds.tldr: The forum highlights practical challenges with AI development tools, particularly Claude Code stability, API proxy security, and account access restrictions. Users are actively sharing educational resources for AIGC workflows while seeking advice on integrating AI into software development and academic research. Concurrently, discussions reflect growing career uncertainty and corporate policy shifts driven by rapid AI adoption.
highlights: Claude Code users report plugin instability, API errors, and subscription hurdles, alongside workplace mandates to use it exclusively.
Technical discussions focus on API proxy abuse mitigation, Gemini CLI tier restrictions, and troubleshooting strict account registration barriers.
Community members share AIGC design courses and development guides while debating AI's impact on career planning and research workflows.

feeds.title: 前沿快讯 - LINUX DO
feeds.tldr: The AI ecosystem is shifting toward usage-based and tiered pricing models, with OpenAI exploring peak/off-peak rates for Codex, Google introducing priority inference tiers for Gemini, and Xiaomi launching a new token-based MiMo plan. Major developer tools are seeing significant updates, including Claude Code v2.1.91 optimizing token consumption and the official release of Qwen 3.6 Plus. Meanwhile, platform and security developments include Slack suspending workspaces in mainland China, a social engineering compromise of the Axios npm package, and new Chinese regulations proposed for digital virtual humans.
highlights: OpenAI explores peak/off-peak pricing for Codex and reduces ChatGPT Business subscription costs.
Xiaomi launches a tiered, token-based MiMo API plan, while Google adds Flex and Priority inference tiers to the Gemini API.
Claude Code v2.1.91 reduces token consumption for edits and increases MCP tool result limits to 500K characters.
The Axios npm package was compromised following a social engineering attack on its maintainer.
Slack suspends workspaces in mainland China, Hong Kong, and Macau, as China drafts new regulations for digital virtual human services.

feeds.title: 王志安 - YouTube
feeds.tldr: The feed covers escalating geopolitical tensions, focusing on the US-Iran conflict, North Korea's accelerated succession planning, and a diplomatic incident involving a Japanese officer at a Chinese embassy. It also highlights intensifying US-China competition in AI, space exploration, and cybersecurity, alongside significant domestic developments in China and Taiwan, including military anti-corruption efforts and high-profile political trials.
highlights: North Korea's leadership transition is accelerating amid Kim Jong Un's health concerns, featuring expanded cyber operations and military cooperation with Russia.
The US-Iran conflict intensifies with reported strikes on Iranian infrastructure, the downing of a US E-3 AWACS, and discussions over joint management of the Strait of Hormuz.

US-China strategic rivalry expands across AI development, lunar exploration, and cybersecurity, while China faces internal military anti-corruption probes and Taiwan navigates high-profile political trials.

feeds.title: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics
feeds.tldr: This bioRxiv collection highlights recent computational methods for analyzing genomic, microbiome, and cytometry data. New tools apply expectation-maximization for fungal abundance estimation, pretrained transformers for cytometry prediction, and k-mer machine learning for SARS-CoV-2 variant classification. A large-scale benchmark also evaluates heritability estimation strategies and their downstream impact on polygenic risk scores.
highlights: EMITS introduces expectation-maximization for fungal ITS abundance estimation using long-read sequencing.
A cytometry foundation model leverages pretrained transformers for interpretable sample-level predictions.
A benchmark of 86 configurations assesses heritability estimation strategies and polygenic risk score performance.

items.title: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing
Towards a Cytometry Foundation Model: Interpretable Sample-level Predictive Modelling via Pretrained Transformers
TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data
Benchmarking Heritability Estimation Strategies Across 86 Configurations and Their Downstream Effect on Polygenic Risk Score Performance

feeds.title: Nature Communications
feeds.tldr: This digest covers recent advances across developmental biology, climate science, materials engineering, and biomedical research. Key findings include the role of Hippo signaling in lung progenitor differentiation, scalable manufacturing techniques for solid-state battery cathodes, and AI-driven insights into primordial protein folding. Additional studies report a 50.28% efficiency in hybrid photovoltaic-thermoelectric systems, mechanisms of oncogene-induced replication stress, and the impact of VRC01 antibodies on HIV transmission dynamics.
highlights: Scalable solvent-free mechanofusion improves cathode coatings for solid-state batteries.
AI protein design demonstrates diverse folds from limited primordial amino acid sets.
Solution-processed PV-TE hybrid systems achieve over 50% energy conversion efficiency.

items.title: Hippo signaling differentially regulates distal progenitor subpopulations and their transitional states to construct the mammalian lungs
items.tldr: Hippo signaling regulates distal SOX9+ lung progenitors to control lung size through bud bifurcation and generate airway and alveolar cell types via transitional states.

items.title: Permafrost landsystems define regional variability in climate change effects on northern environments
items.tldr: Co-developing landform and ecosystem assemblages form permafrost landsystems that vary with geological and climatic legacies, helping predict regional thaw consequences.

items.title: Mechanofusion-derived cathode composite microstructures with scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings for solid state batteries
items.tldr: Solvent-free mechanofusion enables scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings on cathode particles, with coating quality directly linked to macroscopic mixing stresses for solid-state battery manufacturing.

items.title: A highly limited amino acid library from asteroid Bennu yields wide-ranging protein folds
items.tldr: AI-based protein design demonstrates that a limited set of primordial amino acids, constrained by prebiotic and asteroid chemistry data, can produce a broad range of protein folds.

items.title: Probing ultrafast heating and ionization dynamics in solid density plasmas with time-resolved resonant X-ray absorption and emission
items.tldr: Combining resonant X-ray emission spectroscopy and absorption imaging with an XFEL probe achieves sub-picosecond resolution for tracking heating and ionization dynamics in solid-density copper plasmas.

items.title: Solution-processed photovoltaic and thermoelectric hybrid systems with efficiency exceeding 50%
items.tldr: Solution-processed photovoltaic-thermoelectric hybrid systems optimized for heat utilization and current matching experimentally achieve a maximum energy conversion efficiency of 50.28%.

items.title: A conversational multi-agent AI system for automated plant phenotyping
items.tldr: A multi-agent AI system integrates large language models with specialized tools to automate crop phenotype extraction, visualization, and model training, reducing technical barriers in agricultural research.

items.title: Distinct mechanisms of replication stress induced by oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal
items.tldr: Oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 drive replication stress through distinct pathways that converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal, with RAS-induced stalling linked to ROS-mediated replisome alterations.

items.title: Influence of the broadly neutralizing antibody VRC01 on HIV breakthrough virus populations in antibody-mediated prevention trials
items.tldr: Deep sequencing from VRC01 antibody trials reveals frequent multilineage HIV infections and coinfections with sensitive and resistant viruses, indicating the antibody does not alter the transmission bottleneck.

feeds.title: Nature Computational Science
feeds.tldr: This digest covers two recent computational biology studies focusing on biomedical data processing and analysis. The first introduces a self-supervised foundation model that restores medical images without requiring clean reference data. The second evaluates spatial transcriptomics alignment techniques, demonstrating that optimal performance depends on the specific dataset and providing practical guidance for future method development.
highlights: Self-supervised model restores medical images without clean reference data.
Benchmark shows spatial transcriptomics alignment performance is dataset-specific.
Findings offer actionable guidance for future computational biology methods.

items.title: Universal restoration of medical images
items.tldr: HorusEye is a self-supervised foundation model that learns realistic noise patterns directly from X-ray scans to enable robust tomography restoration across various modalities and scanners without requiring clean training data.

items.title: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data
items.tldr: This study benchmarks spatial alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics, finding that optimal performance depends on the specific dataset and offering actionable guidance for future algorithm development.

feeds.title: Nature Health
feeds.tldr: This digest features a Nature Health commentary evaluating the potential of GLP-1 receptor agonists as longevity therapeutics. The authors propose classifying these drugs as transitional interventions but emphasize that current evidence is insufficient. Rigorous clinical testing is required to determine their direct and indirect effects on aging and healthspan.
highlights: GLP-1 receptor agonists are proposed as transitional longevity therapeutics.
Current data is insufficient to confirm direct lifespan benefits.
Rigorous testing is needed to evaluate both direct and indirect health impacts.

items.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
items.tldr: The authors argue that GLP-1 receptor agonists should be classified as transitional longevity therapeutics, but stress that rigorous testing is necessary to fully assess their direct and indirect effects on aging.

feeds.title: Nature Medicine
feeds.tldr: This digest highlights recent Nature Medicine research on viral surveillance, environmental impacts on neurology, and Alzheimer's therapeutic development. Genomic analysis reveals the co-circulation of multiple monkeypox virus clades in the Republic of the Congo, emphasizing the need for enhanced monitoring to prevent recombination. Cross-national exposome studies link social and physical environmental factors to distinct patterns of functional and structural brain aging. Additionally, researchers propose standardized target product profiles to guide and accelerate the development of therapies aimed at delaying or preventing symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease.
highlights: Identification of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 and co-circulation of three clades in the Republic of the Congo.
Association between social exposures and faster functional brain aging, and physical exposures and faster structural brain aging across 34 countries.
Establishment of target product profiles to benchmark and accelerate therapies for delaying or preventing symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease.

items.title: Evidence of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clade Ia, Ib and clade IIb
items.tldr: Whole-genome phylogenetic analysis identified monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo alongside the co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb. The findings underscore the need for enhanced genomic surveillance to monitor potential viral recombination events.

items.title: The exposome of brain aging across 34 countries
items.tldr: An exposome analysis spanning 34 countries found that social environmental factors correlate with accelerated functional brain aging, while physical environmental factors are linked to faster structural brain aging.

items.title: Target product profiles for treatments to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease
items.tldr: The authors outline standardized target product profiles to provide clear developmental benchmarks for therapies aimed at delaying or preventing the clinical onset of Alzheimer’s disease.

feeds.title: Nature Microbiology
feeds.tldr: This digest covers three recent studies in microbial ecology, bacterial genetics, and host-pathogen interactions. Researchers quantified global prokaryotic diversity using large-scale metagenomic data, revealing hundreds of thousands of previously hidden species-level clades primarily within unbinned contigs. Additional work identifies a donor-controlled endonuclease that restricts plasmid transfer through bacterial nanotubes, and demonstrates how cholesterol accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas disrupts macrophage–T cell communication and impairs immune control.
highlights: Metagenomic analysis predicts over 700,000 bacterial and 27,000 archaeal species-level clades globally.
The YokF endonuclease family restricts plasmid exchange between Gram-positive bacteria via intercellular nanotubes.
Cholesterol buildup in TB granulomas disrupts macrophage–CD4+ T cell interactions, weakening anti-tuberculosis immunity.

items.title: Unbinned contigs expand known diversity in the global microbiome
items.tldr: Analysis of over 92,000 metagenomes predicts approximately 705,000 bacterial and 27,000 archaeal species-level clades, with the vast majority discovered in previously unbinned contigs. The study identifies soils and aquatic environments as diversity hotspots and shows that prokaryotic clade sizes follow consistent evolutionary power laws.

items.title: A family of endonucleases blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange
items.tldr: The YokF endonuclease acts as a donor-controlled mechanism to restrict plasmid transfer through intercellular nanotubes in Bacillus subtilis. Functional homologues of this restriction system are present across diverse Gram-positive bacterial species.

items.title: Lipid accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas inhibits macrophage–CD4 + T cell interactions and infection control
items.tldr: Spatial transcriptomics and imaging of human and mouse samples reveal that infection-induced cholesterol accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas disrupts macrophage–CD4+ T cell interactions. This lipid overload impairs the host's ability to control Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
